signals.headline: IMF Explicitly Warns Against Rate Cuts in 2026 | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
IMF Explicitly Warns Against Rate Cuts in 2026 | NO | HIGH confidence**

The International Monetary Fund's Executive Board concluded its 2026 Article IV Consultation on April 2, 2026, stating there is "little room to cut interest rates in 2026."[1] This represents a formal institutional constraint on Fed policy expectations. The IMF projects the fed funds rate will move only from 3.6% to 3.4% acro

signals.headline: Core inflation remains sticky
signals.raw: Core inflation remains sticky.** Core PCE inflation sat at 2.9% in 2025 and the IMF projects it will only decline to 2.6% by end-2026, falling short of the 2% target.[1] Powell himself has been hinting at higher inflation in coming quarters.[1]
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Tariffs are a structural headwind
signals.raw: Tariffs are a structural headwind.** The effective tariff rate is settling at 7-8.5%, and the IMF flagged that tariff increases and trade policy uncertainty "are expected to reduce U.S. activity and create sizable negative spillovers on its trading partners."[1] Energy prices from the Iran conflict are adding upside inflation risk.[1]
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: GDP growth is moderating
signals.raw:
GDP growth is moderating.** The IMF projects U.S. GDP growth of 2.4% in 2026 (up from 2.0% in 2025), but growth is expected to peak this year and then slowly decay toward 1.8%.[1]

The rate cut pathway is effectively frozen absent a "material worsening in labor market prospects alongside a decline in inflationary pressures"—a high bar unlikely to be met given current conditions.[1]
